article.description: — Chet Holmgren scored 35 points and tied a career high with 14 rebounds and the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the short-handed Utah Jazz 119-107 on Wednesday night.
article.headline: Chet Holmgren scores 35, grabs 14 rebounds and Thunder beat Jazz 119-107

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander makes beautiful pass for a basket.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander makes beautiful pass for a basket

article.linkText: Chet Holmgren scores 35, grabs 14 rebounds and Thunder beat Jazz 119-107
article.story: OKLAHOMA CITY -- — Chet Holmgren scored 35 points and tied a career high with 14 rebounds and the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the short-handed Utah Jazz 119-107 on Wednesday night. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 20 of his 31 points in the second half for the Thunder, who have the best record in the Western Conference at 48-20. Alexander became the 13th player in NBA history to score 30 points or more in 50 games. “Credit Utah, they really competed tonight,” Thunder coach Mark Daigneault said. “I thought they were the more physical team after the first six minutes of the game, and that really tested us.” Collin Sexton had 25 points for Utah, which played without injured players Lauri Markkanen and Jordan Clarkson — two of the team’s top three scorers. “I thought we played 42 good minutes tonight,” said Utah coach Will Hardy, whose team has lost three straight and 14 of its last 17 to fall to 29-40. “I was really proud of the team’s effort, competitiveness. I thought the first half was probably as good a half of basketball as we’ve played in a while, which shows some growth and maturity from the team. Turnovers hurt us tonight, for sure.” Utah committed 15 turnovers that resulted in 19 points for Oklahoma City. The Thunder also scored 35 points on second-chance opportunities compared to eight points for the Jazz. Oklahoma City jumped to a 22-11 lead on an alley-oop pass and dunk from Josh Giddey to Holmgren. Utah scored 10 of the next 12 points to pull within 24-21. Holmgren, who finished one point shy of a career high, had nine points and seven rebounds in eight minutes and the Thunder had a 31-25 lead after one quarter. The Jazz outscored Oklahoma City 29-22 in the second to pull in front 54-53 at halftime. Oklahoma City trailed 61-57 early in the third but went on a 11-0 run to pull ahead 68-61. Gilgeous-Alexander scored 15 in the quarter and the Thunder led 82-80 to start the final quarter. “I thought in the second half we showed good persistence,” Daigneault said. “We tried to amp it up on the defensive end. The game didn’t open up for us right away, but we kept at it, and then that group to start the fourth quarter really ignited the rest of the game.” The Thunder took control midway through the fourth, outscoring the Jazz 20-4 at one point to lead 100-86. A 3-pointer by Aaron Wiggins and a pair of 3-point plays by Holmgren — fouled twice on dunks — highlighted the run. “Some shots didn’t fall from the outside, but we did a good job of not just saying ‘We’re a team that’s going to win when we make shots,’ ” said Holmgren, who also blocked three shots. “We found other ways to open up the game and pull away at the end.” The lead grew to 16 points on a 3 by Holmgren and his fifth dunk of the game with 5:50 remaining. Holmgren shot 12 of 18 from the field and the Thunder shot 43 of 88 (48.9%) as a team. UP NEXT Jazz: Visit the Dallas Mavericks on Thursday. Thunder: Visit the Toronto Raptors on Friday. AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/NBA

articles.description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks as the favorites to win the NBA championship.
articles.headline: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Spurs favored vs. Knicks entering Finals

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder are back to being odds-on title favorites after taking a 3-2 series lead over the Spurs.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [608x342]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: New York Knicks guard Landry Shamet (L) defends Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (R) during game 3 of the NBA East Conference Finals between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the New York Knicks in Cleveland, Ohio, USA, 23 May 2026.
images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.
images.name: Knicks/Cavs [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) takes a shot as San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) defends during the first quarter during game two of the western conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Paycom Center.
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander how has even odds to win the Finals MVP.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander  [1296x729]

images.alt: VIctor Wembanyama
images.caption: VIctor Wembanyama poured in 41 points and grabbed 24 boards in the Spurs' thrilling Game 1 win.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Williams [608x342] - Copy

images.caption: The Thunder enter the Western Conference Finals as the odds-on favorite to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.caption: The Spurs took care of business with a big Game 5 win at home against the Timberwolves.

images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.caption: Anthony Edwards returned to the court and helped lead Minnesota to the improbable Game 1 victory.
images.name: Anthony Edwards [1296x729]

images.caption: Cade Cunningham and the Pistons have raced out to a 2-0 series advantage over the Cavs.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.caption: The Knicks have surged to +850 to win the NBA Finals following the first-round exit by the Boston Celtics.
